skill.name: infsh-cli
skill.description: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI for image generation, video creation, LLM calls, web search, and Twitter automation without local GPU.
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 203 potential security issues due to pattern-based detection without semantic analysis. All findings are false positives or standard CLI tool behaviors. The skill is a legitimate CLI wrapper for the inference.sh AI service that executes the `belt` CLI to call AI models. External commands are documented bash commands for the CLI tool. Network calls go to official inference.sh endpoints. Environment access is limited to the INFSH_API_KEY credential. No malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 146 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in markdown files. These are all false positives. The detections are bash command examples in documentation (e.g., 'belt app list', 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh'). Documentation naturally contains command examples. No actual command execution occurs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are markdown documentation containing bash command examples. No actual code execution occurs in documentation files. The skill's purpose is to invoke the belt CLI, so documentation naturally shows CLI commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to Official Service
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged URLs as 'Hardcoded URL' findings. These URLs all point to official inference.sh service endpoints (cli.inference.sh, dist.inference.sh, inference.sh). This is expected behavior for a CLI tool that interfaces with a specific service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are legitimate inference.sh service endpoints. No external third-party domains were detected. The hardcoded URLs are the expected service endpoints for this CLI tool.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequences in Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged '../data/video.mp4' as a potential path traversal concern. This is a false positive - the path traversal sequence appears in documentation examples showing valid input formats that the belt CLI accepts for file uploads. No user-controllable path injection exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged path is a documentation example showing valid relative path format for file uploads. The belt CLI handles file paths safely by accepting local file paths for upload, not serving files from arbitrary locations.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access for Shell Completions
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged ~/.config/fish/completions/infsh.fish and ~/.local/bin/inferencesh as 'Hidden file in home directory'. These are standard shell completion and binary installation paths used by the belt CLI installer. This is legitimate installation behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shell completion files and binary installation in user home directories is standard CLI tool behavior. No malicious hidden files were detected.

content.user_title: Run 250+ AI models via inference.sh CLI
content.value_statement: Developers need GPU-free access to AI models for image generation, video creation, and LLM calls. This skill provides a unified CLI interface to 250+ AI apps through inference.sh, enabling AI capabilities without local infrastructure.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini 3 Pro, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, and Stable Diffusion XL
Create videos with Veo 3.1, Seedance 1.5, Wan 2.5, and OmniHuman avatar
Call LLMs including Claude Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, Gemini 3 Pro, and any OpenRouter model
Perform web search via Tavily Search/Extract and Exa Search/Answer
Automate Twitter/X posting, DMs, follows, likes, and retweets
Upload local files for processing - images, audio, and video
limitations: Requires internet connectivity - all inference runs on remote servers
Execution speed depends on the selected AI model and current load
Requires inference.sh account with valid API key for authentication
Some advanced features and higher usage tiers may require paid subscription

use_cases.title: Generate AI assets for software projects
use_cases.description: Quickly generate images, videos, or 3D models for documentation, marketing materials, or application prototypes without setting up local AI infrastructure.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers building applications with AI-generated content

use_cases.title: Automate research and content workflows
use_cases.description: Use web search and LLM calls to research topics, extract information, and generate content drafts as part of automated workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and researchers needing AI-assisted workflows

use_cases.title: Manage social media automation
use_cases.description: Schedule and automate Twitter/X posts, including text content and media uploads, directly from terminal commands.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and social media managers

prompt_templates.title: Generate an image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the infsh-cli skill to generate an image with the following prompt: "[PROMPT]". Use the flux-dev-lora model for high quality.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to create an image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Generate a video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the infsh-cli skill to create a video using the Veo 3.1 model with this prompt: "[PROMPT]".
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to generate a video from text

prompt_templates.title: Call an LLM for analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the infsh-cli skill to call Claude Sonnet via OpenRouter with this prompt: "[PROMPT]". Get a thoughtful response about [TOPIC].
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs to use an LLM for text analysis or generation

prompt_templates.title: Run a multi-step AI workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the infsh-cli skill to: 1) Search Tavily for [TOPIC], 2) Use the results to generate an image about the key findings, 3) Post the image to Twitter with context about [TOPIC].
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs to chain multiple AI operations

output_examples.input: Generate a landscape image of mountains at sunset
Create a drone shot video flying over a forest
Call Claude to explain quantum computing
output_examples.output:
Running openrouter/claude-sonnet-45

Output: Quantum computing is a type of computation that harnesses quantum mechanical phenomena...
[Full response truncated for display]

Tokens: 847 | Model: claude-sonnet-45 | Latency: 1.2s

best_practices: Install and authenticate before first use: Run 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' then 'belt login'
Use input files for complex operations: Generate sample input with 'belt app sample <app> --save input.json', then edit and run
Check task status for long operations: Use 'belt task get <task-id>' to monitor progress without waiting
anti_patterns: Do not assume offline capability - all inference runs on remote servers requiring internet connectivity
Do not skip the model namespace - always use format 'namespace/app-name' like 'google/veo-3-1-fast'
Do not skip authentication - run 'belt login' or set INFSH_API_KEY before running apps

faq.question: How do I install the belt CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' to install. The script detects your OS and architecture, downloads the correct binary, and verifies its SHA-256 checksum.

faq.question: Do I need an account to use inference.sh?
faq.answer: Yes. Create an account at inference.sh, then run 'belt login' or set your API key with 'export INFSH_API_KEY=your-key'.

faq.question: What models are available?
faq.answer: Over 250 apps including FLUX for images, Veo 3.1 and Seedance for video, Claude/Gemini for LLMs, Tavily/Exa for search, and Twitter automation tools.

faq.question: Can I use local files as input?
faq.answer: Yes. The belt CLI automatically uploads local files when you provide a file path instead of a URL. Supported: images, audio, and video files.

faq.question: How do I track long-running tasks?
faq.answer: Use 'belt task get <task-id>' to check status anytime. Use '--no-wait' flag when submitting to return immediately without waiting for completion.

faq.question: What if a command is not found?
faq.answer: Run 'belt help' to see all available commands. If 'belt' is not found, reinstall the CLI or ensure it is in your PATH.
